meaning: adj.不可动摇的,坚定不移的
examples: More ways to unearth unshakeable confidence.
Deep, profound, moving, unshakeable, upending... but simple.
The Pope has made a openly declared and unshakeable anti-Semite a legitimate bishop, "Politi added."
And I will bring to the White House an unshakeable commitment to Israel's security.
He had unshakeable beliefs and was able to express them effectively, both in America and abroad.
